“不要让它的小巧欺骗了你，因为DJI Mini 2飞行起来很自信，而且有4K的视频录制功能。”
我以为大疆已经不再发布今年的公告了。在DJI Mavic Air 2和Pocket 2发布后，我们似乎不太可能在2020年听到这家无人机制造商的其他消息，但事实证明，在假期之前，它还有最后一款令消费者兴奋的产品。是的，这是另一架无人机！
无人机通常不会每年更新一次，这就是为什么大疆Mini 2的声明令人惊讶的原因。由于重量249克，这架轻型无人机继续在美国联邦航空管理局(FAA)的雷达下飞行，这使它成为初学者的理想之选，因为它不需要注册。不过，更重要的是，它直截了当地解决了前身的一些缺点。
在4K视频录制在智能手机和相机中都是标配的时代，去年的Mavic Mini达到了2.7k的最高分辨率，这让人有点失望。嗯，这是Mini 2解决的第一个问题，它号称有合适的4K视频捕捉功能。虽然4K的帧速率最高可达每秒30帧，但大疆已经将Mavic Mini的比特率从40 Mbps提高到了Mini 2的100 Mbps。
这一切意味着什么？我对额外的分辨率特别满意，因为它在POST中为我提供了一些额外的回旋余地--比如当我以1080p格式输出时，可以切换场景。与此同时，对于那些在4K屏幕上观看视频和剪辑的人来说，4K分辨率意味着这一次没有必要进行升级。
细节出人意料地丰富和清爽，但最好的效果出现在白天和下午光线充足的时候。颜色具有均匀、中性的色调-不会太饱和而不能在开机自检时使用。然而，太阳一落山，它的性能就会崩溃，除非场景中有很强的光源，否则通常会显得昏暗、黑暗和不可用。这在一定程度上是因为传感器没有变化，一个1200万像素的1/2.3英寸传感器安装在一个3轴万向节上。
动态范围的性能没有接近我在Mavic Air 2上得到的水平，但在POST中进行一些调整可以绘制出一些细节，否则这些细节就会消失在阴影中。摄影师会欣赏Mini 2的静态捕捉性能，但就像视频一样，它在微光和动态范围内也很挣扎。
如果你曾经驾驶过Mavic Mini，你就会知道视频传输是如何容易受到干扰的。这当然很烦人，但如果持续很长时间，也会让人害怕。幸运的是，由于大疆的OcuSync 2.0传输技术，大疆Mini 2缓解了人们的担忧。这款控制器和Mavic Air 2的一模一样。它的尺寸要大一些，但我喜欢我的智能手机紧贴在控制器上的方式--而不是Mavic Mini的控制器在底部。
有了OcuSync 2.0，无人机和控制器之间的连接更加可靠。事实上，在我的飞行过程中，直播视频几乎不会卡顿。虽然我确实设法将它的距离推到了离我所在位置1000英尺以上，但大疆表示，它的射程可以达到10公里(6.2英里)。每当我驾驶无人机时，没有什么比延迟问题更糟糕的了，所以OcuSync 2.0为我提供了更灵敏的Mini 2控制，这是有保证的。
我对驾驶Mavic Mini最大的担忧之一是将它的航程推得更远，因为它的视频传输可能会受到干扰。而Mini 2则不具备这些功能。
在有风的条件下飞行总是有风险的，但当你面对像Mavic Mini这样的轻型无人机时，风险就更大了。我数不清有多少次因为刮风而取消飞行。网上也有几个视频显示，业主们对此做出了反应，因为大风使他们漂离了自己所在的位置。尽管我仍然很谨慎，但DJI Mini 2对风的驾驭能力要好得多。
在驾驶Mini 2的第一天，泽西海岸就遭遇了时速17英里的大风。值得庆幸的是，大疆升级了Mini 2的发动机，以更好地抵御时速高达24英里的风。过去，每当风速达到每小时12英里时，我都会取消乘坐Mavic Mini的航班。在空中，很明显Mini 2正在努力保持稳定，虽然看起来它像是在嘎嘎作响，但视频片段仍然保持着黄油般的流畅。
为了真正测试它的性能，我决定看看它是如何应对逆风飞行的。令人震惊的是，它的表现就像冠军一样--即使在正常模式下也是如此。切换到运动模式增加了功率，这使得它可以轻松地切断风力。它的最高时速仍然达到每小时14英里左右，但从悬停位置的加速度可以明显看出马达的动力更强了。
你担心这些升级后的发动机会缩短飞行时间吗？你不必担心，因为它的电池续航时间增加到了31分钟，比之前的版本长了一分钟。不幸的是，Mini 2使用了不同类型的电池，所以你将无法使用Mavic Mini的现有电池包。
我不得不承认，我被Mavic Air 2宠坏了，特别是它增强的HDR捕捉、4K 60FPS捕捉、8K Hyperapse和小行星快拍模式。公平地说，Mini 2确实提供了一些巧妙的电影镜头，可以为我自动驾驶无人机-比如无人机、回旋镖和圆形快拍模式。然而，Mini 2还有一个很大的不足之处，那就是避障。Mavic Air 2在其身上安装了各种传感器来探测障碍物，因此它可以在飞行中停止并试图避开它们。Mini 2不具备这一点，这意味着我需要时刻了解周围环境--外加所需的视线距离。
此外，Mini 2也没有自动跟踪模式可以跟踪我，所以用控制器手动驾驶仍然是拍摄镜头的主要方式。这不是一件可怕的事情，但当你被Mavic Air 2的Active Track 3.0系统宠坏的时候，我不介意在湖边骑车时跟着我，而不用担心它会撞到什么东西。
此外，在驾驶无人机时，DJI Fly应用程序也有几次在我身上崩溃。我怀疑这是因为它不是最终的软件，但幸运的是我重新启动了它，并回到了我停止的地方。
选择DJI Mini 2的理由有很多，特别是如果你是第一次使用无人机或者是一名爱好者。它标价449美元，与400美元的Mavic Mini相比，这是一个简单的推荐。我建议选择售价599美元的Fly More Combo，因为它附带了一些好东西，使它成为一个完美的套餐。
它不仅配备了三节电池、一个充电集线器和额外的螺旋桨，而且我更喜欢它而不是Fly More Combo，后者包括去年的Mavic Mini，因为它有一个合适的手提箱(包括肩带)和一个环绕无人机的螺旋桨护罩。
Mini 2完美吗？不完全是，但它仍然是一架令人惊叹的微型无人机，因为它的重量为249克，不需要联邦航空局注册。4K录音的加入使其成为该领域的一名称职玩家，但真正使这款产品大大优于其前身的是对驾驶它的信心-这要归功于OcuSync 2.0和升级的发动机。满怀信心地飞行总是一件好事，Mini 2的响应性让我确信了这一点。
同样的全塑料结构在这里仍然很明显，这肯定让它感觉有点脆弱。我不指望它能很好地处理坠机事件，所以谨慎飞行势在必行。你还需要格外小心地打包旅行，因为它的手感很脆弱。
你可以以低价购买一台带有Mini 2的大疆护理更新。由于水损坏和碰撞，它在一年内提供最多两个更换装置。
在这个价位？不怎么有意思。但是，如果你愿意花更多的钱，DJI Mavic Air 2是所有级别的全能无人机，这要归功于它的安全功能、卓越的照片/视频性能和先进的跟踪系统。但你花了将近双倍的799美元才能拿到它。
是。考虑到它是多么容易驾驶，以及4K视频录制，电影快速拍摄选项，以及紧凑的尺寸，这是一个彻头彻尾的便宜货。与以前不同的是，由于OcuSync 2.0及其升级的发动机，我对驾驶它更有信心了。对于那些想要提升自己的游戏水平而不是为了好玩而飞行的人来说，这是一款理想的首发无人机。
note_en:
“Don't let its small size fool you because the DJI Mini 2 flies confidently and features 4K video recording.”
  I thought DJI was done with announcements for the year. After the launch of the  DJI Mavic Air 2 and  Pocket 2, it seemed unlikely that we would hear anything else from the drone maker for 2020, but it turns out that it has one last exciting product for consumers before the holidays. And yes, it’s another drone!
 Drones don’t typically receive yearly refreshes, which is exactly why the DJI Mini 2’s announcement comes as a surprise. The lightweight drone continues to fly under the Federal Aviation Administration’s (FAA) radar because of its 249-gram weight, making it ideal for first timers because it doesn’t require registration. More importantly, though, it squarely addresses some of the  shortcomings of its predecessor.
  In an age where 4K video recording is standard among smartphones and cameras, it was a bit of a letdown that last year’s Mavic Mini topped out at 2.7K resolution. Well, that’s the first item on the list addressed by the Mini 2, which boasts proper 4K video capture. While the frame rate at 4K tops out at 30 frames per second (FPS), DJI has bumped up the bit rate from 40 Mbps on the Mavic Mini to 100 Mbps for the Mini 2.
  What does it all mean? I’m particularly pleased with the extra resolution, since it offers some additional wiggle room in post for me — such as being able to crop in and out of a scene when I export it in 1080p. Meanwhile, for those who are watching videos and clips on 4K screens, the 4K resolution means that there’s no upscaling necessary this time around.
 Details are expectedly plentiful and crisp, but the best results are found during the daytime and afternoon when lighting is abundant. Colors have an even, neutral tone to them — never too saturated to work with in post. However, its performance crumbles as soon as the sun goes down, often appearing murky, dark, and unusable unless there are strong sources of light in the scene. That’s partly because there’s no change with the sensor, a 12-megapixel 1/2.3-inch sensor perched on a 3-axis gimbal.
  Dynamic range performance isn’t close to the level I got with the Mavic Air 2, but some tweaking in post can draw out details that are otherwise lost in the shadows. Photographers will appreciate the Mini 2’s still-capture performance, but just like video, it struggles under low light and dynamic range.
  If you’ve ever piloted the Mavic Mini, you know how video transmission can be susceptible to interference. It’s annoying for sure, but also scary if it’s prolonged. Luckily, the DJI Mini 2 eases concerns thanks to DJI’s OcuSync 2.0 transmission technology. The controller is an exact facsimile of the Mavic Air 2’s. It’s larger in size, but I like how my smartphone is gripped on top of the controller — as opposed to the bottom with the Mavic Mini’s controller.
  With OcuSync 2.0, there’s more reliability with the connectivity between the drone and controller. Live video, in fact, rarely stuttered during my flights. And while I did manage to push its distance to over 1,000 feet from my location, DJI says that it’s capable of reaching a 10 kilometer range (6.2 miles). There’s nothing worse than latency issues whenever I’m piloting a drone, so it’s assuring that OcuSync 2.0 provides me with more responsive controls on the Mini 2.
  One of my biggest fears about flying the Mavic Mini is pushing its range farther away because of the potential of interference with its video transmission. There’s none of that here with the Mini 2.
  Flying under windy conditions is always risky, but it’s more profound when you’re dealing with a lightweight drone like the Mavic Mini. I can’t tell you how many times I’ve aborted flying it because of windy conditions. There are also  several videos online of owners reacting to it floating away from their location because of strong winds. Even though I’m still cautious, the DJI Mini 2 handles the wind a lot better.
 On the first day of flying the Mini 2, the Jersey Shore was being blasted by 17 mph winds. Thankfully, DJI upgraded the motors in the Mini 2 to better withstand winds up to 24 mph. I’ve always aborted flights with the Mavic Mini in the past whenever winds reached 12 mph. In the air, it’s clear that the Mini 2 was working hard to maintain stability, and while it looked like it was rattling around, the video footage remained buttery smooth.
  In truly testing out its performance, I decided to see how it handles flying against the wind. Shockingly enough, it performed like a champ — even in normal mode. Switching over to sport mode increases the power, which allowed it to cut through the wind with ease. Its top speed still peaks at about 14 mph, but the acceleration from a hovering position is evident that the motors are stronger.
 Are you worried that these upgraded motors will yield a shorter flight time? You don’t need to worry because its battery life has been increased to 31 minutes, which is a minute better than its predecessor. Unfortunately, the Mini 2 uses a different kind of battery, so you won’t be able to use existing packs you may have from the Mavic Mini.
  I have to confess that I’ve been spoiled by the Mavic Air 2, specifically its enhanced HDR capture, 4K 60 FPS capture, 8K hyperlapse, and asteroid quickshot mode. To be fair, the Mini 2 does offer some neat cinematic shots that autonomously pilots the drone for me — like dronie, boomerang, and circle quickshot modes. However, the single area that’s still greatly lacking with the Mini 2 is obstacle avoidance. The Mavic Air 2 features various sensors on its body to detect obstacles, so it can stop midflight and try to avoid them. You get none of that with the Mini 2, which means that I need to be cognizant of my surroundings at all times — plus that required line of sight distance.
  There’s also no automatic tracking mode that would allow the Mini 2 to follow me, so manual piloting with the controller is still the main way of capturing footage. It’s not a terrible thing, but when you’ve been spoiled by the Mavic Air 2’s Active Track 3.0 system, it’s something I wouldn’t mind having to follow me while biking around a lake, without that fear of it colliding with something.
 Also, there were a couple of times when the DJI Fly app crashed on me while flying the drone. I suspect it’s because it’s not final software, but luckily I restarted it and got back to where I left off.
   There are many reasons to pick up the DJI Mini 2, especially if you’re a first-time drone flyer or an enthusiast. Sporting a  price tag of $449, it’s an easy recommendation over the $400 Mavic Mini. I’d recommend picking up the $599 Fly More Combo instead because it comes with a few goodies that make it a sound package.
 Not only does it come with three batteries, a charging hub, and extra propellers, but I prefer it over the Fly More Combo that included last year’s Mavic Mini because it comes with a proper carrying case (shoulder strap included) and a propeller guard that wraps around the drone.
 Is the Mini 2 perfect? Not quite, but it’s still an amazing, tiny drone that doesn’t require FAA registration because of its 249-gram weight. The addition of 4K recording makes it a competent player in the space, but what truly makes this offering substantially superior to its predecessor is the confidence instilled in flying it — thanks to OcuSync 2.0 and the upgraded motors. Flying with confidence is always a good thing, and the Mini 2 assures me of that with its responsiveness.
  The same all-plastic construction is still evident here, which certainly makes it feel a bit on the fragile side. I don’t expect it to handle crashes all that well, so flying cautiously is imperative. You’ll also want to be extra cautious packing it up for travel because of its fragile feel.
 You can purchase a  DJI Care Refresh with the Mini 2 at a cos. It provides up to two replacement units within one year due to water damage and collisions.
  At this price point? Not really. But, if you’re willing to spend more, the  DJI Mavic Air 2 is the all-around drone for all levels thanks to its safety features, superior photo/video performance, and advanced tracking system. But you’re paying nearly double at $799 to pick it up.
  Yes. It’s a downright bargain given how easy it is to pilot, as well as the 4K video recording, cinematic quickshot options, and compact size. Unlike before, I have more confidence flying it because of OcuSync 2.0 and its upgraded motors. It’s the ideal starter drone for those looking to step up their game beyond flying for fun.
title: DJI推出了售价449美元的Mini 2，配备了升级的摄像头，可进行4K 30fps的录制；OcuSync 2.0，具有10公里的视频传输范围，声称飞行时间为31分钟
title_en: DJI launches the $449 Mini 2 with upgraded camera for 4K 30fps recording, OcuSync 2.0 with a 10 km video transmission range, claims 31 mins of flight time

note:
WhatsApp表示，从今天开始，消失信息将在全球安卓和iOS系统中推广。虽然它一开始只有7天的寿命，但它已经在考虑玩弄时间限制了。
一位发言人说：“我们将密切关注人们对它的使用和喜爱程度的反馈，看看将来是否需要调整。”“就目前而言，我们从7天开始，因为这感觉像是在基于全球文本的对话所需的实用工具和事物不会永远存在的感觉之间取得了很好的平衡。”
需要说明的是，无论邮件是否被阅读，7天的限制都将存在。(消失的消息时钟在消息发送时开始计时，就像Telegram等其他应用程序一样。)。
“目前的设计方式是让发件人相信，他们的邮件在7天后就会消失。”这些信息没有被看到的概念，也没有让它们消失的概念，所以无论阅读状态如何，它们都会消失。“这位发言人说。
用户可以打开直接消息功能，但在群组中，只有管理员才能启用消失消息功能。
虽然今天是“官方”声明，但眼光敏锐的WhatsApp观察者几天前就发现该公司在这一功能上发布了常见问题。而对这一功能的测试实际上早在今年3月就开始出现--也恰如其分地消失了。
2017年，该公司首次涉足这一想法，推出了Status--Snapchat故事功能的加密克隆，允许人们以文本和/或GIF的形式设置关于自己的简短更新，本质上是让他们所有的联系人在设定的24小时内都能看到“个人资料”，状态存在于应用程序中他们自己的标签中，与你的聊天分开。
目前还不清楚状态功能有多受欢迎：我们已经联系过了。有趣的是，我看到年轻人经常使用它，在那里，它是更新你在Facebook或Snapchat上状态的精简版，而老年人则不那么用。
WhatsApp表示，开发者花了这么长时间才将这一短暂的功能带到更广泛的聊天体验中，部分原因是这款应用的加密功能：
这位发言人说：“(端到端加密)是我们花了这么长时间才实施这一功能的部分原因，因为我们想保留WhatsApp用户期望和喜爱的e2e功能。”
短暂性是近年来消息传递中最激进和最具粘性的特征之一--它已经存在了近十年。可以说，它是这一类型的热门热门应用之一Snapchat的决定性功能--它如此之多，以至于它的克隆在其他一些应用中也出现了，从Signal和Telegram等首先关注隐私的应用，到那些针对更随意的消费者受众的应用，比如今天的WhatsApp。
有迹象表明，Facebook可能也在寻求在其稳定的其他应用程序中推出这一功能。今年早些时候，它在Instagram上测试了消失的信息，现在Instagram已经正式运行。然而，Messenger最新的短信消失测试似乎是在2015年进行的，但它确实有一个“秘密”短信功能(于2016年推出)，可以让发送者控制一条短信保留多长时间。然而，这并不完全等同于主消息传递体验中包含的内容。
新的即时通讯功能消失的同时，该应用程序还增加了其他一些引人注目的功能，这些功能似乎是为了帮助实现赋予用户更多控制权的总体目的。
本周早些时候，WhatsApp宣布，它将在应用程序中启用一项新的存储功能：具体地说，这是一种更简单的方式，可以控制你如何以及在哪里实时发送照片和其他媒体。这对该应用程序的活跃用户(但可能不是深度用户)尤其重要，他们发现自己的存储空间正被朋友和熟人通过该应用程序发送的无害的GIF、照片和视频吞噬。
与此同时，它还一直在加强向企业提供的服务，并测试向企业收费的商业模式，这是坚守不在服务中投放广告的承诺的一种方式。考虑到Facebook在这之前的几年里一直没有做太多的功能更新，这可能会对它造成不利影响，因为这款应用面临着一系列争议，因为它是如何被一些人滥用的。最近，我们看到这款应用增加了更多功能，这也是值得注意的。
与存储更改一样，默认情况下不会为用户打开新的消失功能：您必须主动更改设置。
note_en:
Disappearing messages are being rolled out globally across Android and iOS starting today, WhatsApp said. While it’s starting with a 7 day lifespan, it is already looking at playing around with the time limits.
 “We will keep an eye on feedback about how people are using it and liking it and see if it needs adjusting in the future,” a spokesperson said. “For now we are starting with seven days, because it feels like a nice balance between the utility you need for global text based conversations and the feeling of things not sticking around forever.”
  And just to be clear, the 7-day limit will exist regardless of whether the message gets read or not. (The disappearing message clock starts counting when the message is sent, as it does on other apps like Telegram.)
 “The way it’s currently designed is to give the sender confidence that after 7 days their message is gone. The messages have no concept of being seen, for them to disappear, so they will disappear regardless of read status,” said the spokesperson.
 Users can turn on the feature for direct messages, but in groups it’s the admin that has to enable disappearing messages for it to work.
 Although today is the “official” announcement, eagle-eyed WhatsApp watchers had spotted the company  posting FAQs on the feature some days ago. And tests of the feature actually first started to appear — and,  fittingly, disappear — as early as  March of this year.
  In  2017, the company first dabbled in the idea with the launch of Status — an encrypted clone of Snapchat’s Stories feature, which let people set up short updates on themselves — in the form of some text and/or a GIF — as essentially a “profile” for all of their contacts to see for a set period of 24 hours, with the Statuses existing in their own tab in the app separate from your chats.
 It’s not clear how popular the Status feature is these days: we’ve reached out to ask. Anecdotally, I’ve seen younger people using it  a lot, where it lives as a proxy, pared-down version of updating your status on Facebook or Snapchat, older people less so.
 WhatsApp said that one of the reasons it’s taken its developers so long to bring the ephemeral feature to the wider chat experience is in part because of the encrypted aspect of the app:
 “[End-to-end encryption] was partly why it took us so long to implement this feature, because we wanted to retain the e2e capabilities that  WhatsApp users expect and love,” the spokesperson said.
  Ephemerality has been one of the most radical and sticky features in messaging in years — it has now been around for close to a decade. And it has arguably been the defining feature for one of the runaway, viral hits of the genre, Snapchat — so much so that clones of the feature have popped up in a number of other apps, from those focused first and foremost on privacy like Signal and Telegram, through to those that are aimed at more casual consumer audiences, like WhatsApp today.
 And there could be signs that Facebook is may be looking to roll this out in other apps in its stable, too. Earlier this year it  tested disappearing messages in Instagram, which  now works officially. However in Messenger the most recent tests for disappearing text seem to be  from 2015, but it does have a “ secret” messaging feature (launched in  2016) that lets the sender control how long a message remains. This however is not quite the same as something baked into the main messaging experience.
 The new disappearing messaging feature is coming amid some other notable additions in the app that appear to be in aid of the general purpose of giving more control to users.
 Earlier this week, WhatsApp announced that it would enable a new storage feature in the app: specifically, an easier way to control how and where photos and other media that you are sent live. This is especially important for active (but perhaps not deep) users of the app, who find their storage is getting gobbled up by innocuous GIFs, photos and videos sent over the app by friends and acquaintances.
 At the same time, it has also been beefing up the services it  offers to businesses, and testing out business models for charging them, one way to stick to their commitment not to put ads into the service. The acceleration we’re seeing in adding more features to the app in recent times is notable, too, given how Facebook didn’t do much to update the features for years at a time before this, possibly to its detriment as the app faced a  string of controversies over how it got abused by some.
 As with the storage changes, the new disappearing feature will not be switched on for users by default: you have to proactively change the settings.
title: WhatsApp推出了短暂的信息、照片和视频，这些信息、照片和视频在七天后就会消失，从今天开始在iOS和Android上全球推出
title_en: WhatsApp launches ephemeral messages, photos, and videos that disappear after seven days, rolling out on iOS and Android globally starting today

note:
迈阿密--在总统竞选结果还没有完全明朗之前，这些帖子在选举之夜激增。他们用西班牙语错误地重复了一遍又一遍“罗巴多”(Robado)：特朗普总统的胜利被剥夺了。他赢得了亚利桑那州。乔治·索罗斯资助了暴力的“反法骚乱”。
社交媒体向拉丁裔发出的毫无根据的信息，试图让小约瑟夫·R·拜登(Joseph R.Biden Jr.)的选举和选举结果失去合法性。周二晚间和周三在网上流传，这是一场造谣运动的一部分，目的是在投票过程中削弱拉美裔人对投票的信心。
在选举日前夕，西班牙语的假新闻试图让拉美裔人反对黑人生命也是命(Black Lives Matter)，并将拜登与社会主义联系在一起。专家说，这些策略可能会压低拉美裔选民的选票。现在投票已经完成，这些猖獗的谎言只会吸引更多的观众--包括不太熟悉美国民主制度的移民。这些谎言的主旨在于，选举受到“操纵”，不利于特朗普。
追踪虚假信息的非营利组织Avaaz的主管法迪·库兰(Fadi Quran)说，“这些虚假信息的叙述正在帮助这个国家进一步陷入混乱和困惑。”他称造谣运动为“民主紧急状态”。“这个国家最脆弱的社区正在付出最高的代价，”他说。
几周来，官员和选举安全专家一直在为人们普遍预期的一场被黑客和错误信息破坏的选举做好准备。他们将矛头对准了俄罗斯熟悉的对手，几周前，俄罗斯曾被发现在墨西哥和委内瑞拉雇佣人员推送Instagram和Facebook内容。
在伊朗人冒充极右翼骄傲男孩组织的成员威胁民主党人不投票后，官员和研究人员担心伊朗的干预。周三，美国司法部(Justice Department)宣布取缔数十个针对美国人的伊朗宣传网站。微软(Microsoft)和美国网络司令部(United States Cyber Command)分别将目标对准了俄罗斯网络罪犯的黑客基础设施，以阻止可能冻结选举系统的勒索软件攻击。
但在周二，随着选举日相对安然无恙地过去，他们松了一口气。一位政府官员说，这只是互联网上的又一个周二。
然而，24小时后，Facebook和Twitter似乎忽视了针对说西班牙语的美国人的虚假信息泛滥。拥有大量追随者的西班牙语账户错误地说，特朗普已经提前获胜，社交媒体正在审查他的胜利，拜登在作弊。
拥有大量追随者的Twitter账户宣扬了一个被揭穿的阴谋论，即亚利桑那州马里科帕县的选举工作人员给了特朗普选民选票扫描仪无法检测到的钢笔。这一理论被美国一些知名保守派人士采用。其他人则声称，由亿万富翁索罗斯资助的武装抗议者正在占领美国国会大厦。
到了周三，像古兰这样的虚假信息专家将大量西班牙语虚假信息比作紧急情况，并呼吁社交媒体平台追溯性地通知任何参与这些内容的人，这些说法是虚假的。
虚假信息的影响范围很广。在短短24小时内，西班牙语虚假信息产生的流量甚至超过了四年前克里姆林宫支持的俄罗斯互联网研究机构(Russia Internet Research Agency)的干预行动。
在Facebook上，一个名为“卡帕乔先生”(Mr Capacho En Vivo)的哥伦比亚账号上发布的一段视频指责Twitter审查特朗普的胜利，视频的浏览量已经超过50万次--远远超过2016年大选前俄罗斯巨魔用虚假的“黑人生命也是命”(Black Lives Matter)广告产生的流量。该账号有4万名粉丝。
哥伦比亚的这个账号以全球财经和政治新闻页面的形式进行营销，推动了QAnon的阴谋，比如毫无根据地认为民主党领袖是全球撒旦猥亵儿童阴谋的一部分。周一，同一个账号发布了一段经过编辑、具有误导性的视频，视频中拜登抚摸儿童，谎称自己是“超级捕食者”。截至周三，这段视频已经被观看了大约4.5万次。
另一位西班牙语影响力人物西罗·戈麦斯·莱瓦(Ciro Gómez Leyva)拥有200多万YouTube订阅者，他在周二晚些时候发布了一段视频，声称150名Antifa成员带着“防毒面具、武器和盾牌”来到华盛顿。虽然抗议者确实在白宫外游行，但没有证据表明涉及武器。
在YouTube西班牙语频道Campecheando上，一名主持人警告观众不要相信拜登在选举人团计票中超过特朗普的消息。截至周三，已有约16万人观看了这段视频。
周二，在另一个拥有50多万订户的西班牙语频道Informativo G24上，主持人将民主党人比作纳粹。这段视频的点击量约为35万次。
在迈阿密，西班牙语广播节目“卡达·塔尔孔·卡里内斯·蒙卡达和奥古斯丁·阿科斯塔”(Cada Tarde Tarde con Carinés Moncada y Agustín Acosta)周二晚些时候驳斥了共和党民调观察人员被禁止观察宾夕法尼亚州投票点的说法。
9月份，该节目的联合主持人之一卡琳娜·A·蒙卡达(Carinés A.Moncada)宣扬了一种阴谋论，即“黑人生命也是命”(Black Lives Matter)的联合创始人参与了魔鬼崇拜。
在选举日之前，很难(如果不是不可能)知道虚假声明对拉美裔选民的影响有多大。但大部分错误信息的中心是南佛罗里达州，那里居住着一个多元化的拉丁裔社区，他们中的许多人逃离了左翼威权政权，接受了关于社会主义的信息。
周二，迈阿密-戴德县的选举结果公布时，特朗普的表现超出了预期。拉美裔选民的大力支持帮助他轻松赢得佛罗里达州，并使共和党人得以在该州翻转两个国会席位。
佛罗里达州萨拉索塔的哥伦比亚政治分析人士胡安·巴勃罗·萨拉斯(Juan Pablo Salas)说，他担心涉及波哥大、迈阿密和华盛顿的错误信息可能是哥伦比亚和美国的右翼利益集团之间的协同行动，“实质上是为了把我们的哥伦比亚裔美国人社区变成极右翼势力攻击的矛尖。”
“他们投入了大量资金，把南佛罗里达州变成了竞选堡垒，”萨拉斯在谈到共和党和哥伦比亚前总统阿尔瓦罗·乌里韦(Allvaro Uribe)所在的政党时说，乌里韦与迈阿密的许多共和党人关系密切。
周二晚间，佛罗里达国际大学政治学教授爱德华多·A·加迈拉(Eduardo A.Gamarra)同意在哥伦比亚的电视新闻网络NTN24上分析这次选举。他发现自己正在与前联合国代表奥马尔·布拉·埃斯科瓦尔(Omar Bula Ecotbar)进行辩论，埃斯科瓦尔因称索罗斯控制着民主党而臭名昭著，这一说法受到了QAnon的支持。
加马拉博士说，他否认了布拉埃斯科瓦尔关于选举舞弊的毫无根据的指控，并在采访结束后给电视网打了电话。
“我说：‘别再这样对我了。对你的客人做背景调查。‘“他说。“这是不负责任的。”
周三，该节目的制片人没有立即回复记者的置评请求。
南佛罗里达州的民主党拉丁裔问题策略师伊芙琳·佩雷斯-韦尔迪亚(Evelyn Pérez-Verdía)说，周二，她花了四个小时试图驳斥西班牙语电台关于温和派拜登是左翼激进分子的虚假说法。
她说：“在那四个小时里，共和党人20次称他为社会主义者。”“激进的五次，卡斯特罗-查维斯塔”--指的是古巴的菲德尔·卡斯特罗(Fidel Castro)和委内瑞拉的乌戈·查韦斯(Hugo Chávez)--“三次”。重复一个谎言，重复它，直到它变成事实。“
note_en:
MIAMI — The posts proliferated on election night before anything remotely definitive was known about the results of the presidential race. “Robado,” they falsely repeated again and again in Spanish:  President Trump was being robbed of a victory. He had won Arizona. George Soros was funding violent “antifa riots.”
 The baseless social media messages to Latinos trying to delegitimize the election and the results for  Joseph R. Biden Jr. circulated online on Tuesday night and into Wednesday, part of a disinformation campaign to undermine Latino confidence in the vote as it unfolded.
 Ahead of Election Day,  false news in Spanish tried to turn Latinos against Black Lives Matter and tie Mr. Biden to socialism, tactics that experts said could depress the Hispanic vote. Now that voting is complete, the rampant falsehoods have only garnered larger audiences — including among immigrants less familiar with the institutions of American democracy. The gist of the falsehoods is that the election is “rigged” against Mr. Trump.
 “These misinformation narratives are helping plunge the country further into chaos and confusion,” said Fadi Quran, a director at Avaaz, a nonprofit that tracks disinformation. He called the disinformation campaigns a “democratic emergency.” “The most vulnerable communities in the country are paying the highest price,” he said.
 For weeks, officials and election security experts braced for what was widely expected to be an election marred by hacking and misinformation. They zeroed in on familiar adversaries in Russia, which weeks earlier had been caught hiring people in Mexico and Venezuela to push out Instagram and Facebook content.
 The officials and researchers worried about interference from Iran after  Iranians posing as members of the far-right Proud Boys group threatened Democrats not to vote. The Justice Department on Wednesday announced  a takedown of dozens of Iranian propaganda sites targeting Americans. And Microsoft and United States Cyber Command separately took aim at Russian cybercriminals’ hacking infrastructure in an effort to stave off the kinds of ransomware attacks that could freeze up election systems.
 But on Tuesday, they breathed a sigh of relief as Election Day passed relatively unscathed. It was, one administration official said, just another Tuesday on the internet.
 Yet 24 hours later, it appeared Facebook and Twitter might have overlooked the deluge of disinformation targeting Spanish-speaking Americans. Spanish-language accounts with huge followings falsely said that Mr. Trump had secured an early victory, that social media was censoring his win and that Mr. Biden was cheating.
 Twitter accounts with large followings pushed a debunked conspiracy theory, adopted by some prominent American conservatives, that election workers in Maricopa County, Ariz., had given Trump voters pens that could not be detected by ballot scanners. Others claimed that armed protesters funded by the billionaire Mr. Soros were taking over the U.S. Capitol.
 By Wednesday, disinformation experts like Mr. Quran likened the flood of Spanish-language disinformation to an emergency and called on social media platforms to retroactively inform anyone who engaged with the content that the claims were false.
 The reach of the disinformation is vast. In just 24 hours, Spanish-language disinformation was generating traffic that eclipsed even the interference campaign by the Kremlin-backed Russian Internet Research Agency four years ago.
 On Facebook, a video posted to a seven-month-old Colombian account called Mr. Capacho en Vivo, with 40,000 followers, accused Twitter of censoring Mr. Trump’s victory and had already been viewed more than 500,000 times — far more traffic than Russian trolls generated with fake Black Lives Matter ads ahead of the 2016 election.
 Marketed as a global finance and political news page, the Colombian account pushed QAnon conspiracies, such as the baseless belief that leading Democrats are part of a global cabal of Satanist child molesters. On Monday, the same account posted an edited, misleading video of Mr. Biden touching children, falsely claiming that he was a “superpredator.” By Wednesday, the video had been watched about 45,000 times.
 Another Spanish-language influencer, Ciro Gómez Leyva, who has more than two million YouTube subscribers, posted a video late Tuesday in which he claimed that 150 members of antifa were descending on Washington with “gas masks, arms and shields.” While protesters did march outside the White House, there was no evidence that weapons were involved.
  On the Spanish-language YouTube channel Campechaneando, a host warned viewers not to believe news that Mr. Biden had overtaken Mr. Trump in the Electoral College count. Some 160,000 people had watched the video by Wednesday.
 On another Spanish-language channel, Informativo G24, with more than 500,000 subscribers, hosts compared Democrats to Nazis on Tuesday. The video was seen some 350,000 times.
 In Miami, a Spanish-language radio show, “Cada Tarde con Carinés Moncada y Agustín Acosta,” pushed debunked claims late Tuesday that Republican poll watchers had been barred from observing voting locations in Pennsylvania.
 In September, one of the show’s co-hosts, Carinés A. Moncada, had pushed a conspiracy theory that a co-founder of Black Lives Matter was involved in devil worship.
 It is difficult, if not impossible, to know how much false claims influenced Latino voters ahead of Election Day. But the hub of much of that misinformation was South Florida, home to a diverse community of Latinos, many of whom fled left-wing authoritarian regimes and are receptive to messaging about socialism.
 When the results from  Miami-Dade County came in on Tuesday, Mr. Trump outperformed expectations. Robust support from Hispanic voters  helped him win Florida easily and allowed Republicans to  flip two congressional seats in the state.
 Juan Pablo Salas, a Colombian political analyst in Sarasota, Fla., said he worried that the misinformation involving Bogotá, Miami and Washington could be a coordinated effort between right-wing interests in Colombia and the U.S., “to essentially turn our Colombian-American community into the tip of the spear of the offense played by the extreme right.”
 “They’ve invested a lot of money in turning South Florida into a campaign bastion,” Mr. Salas said of the Republican Party and the party of former President Álvaro Uribe of Colombia, who is close with many Miami Republicans.
 On Tuesday night, Eduardo A. Gamarra, a professor of political science at Florida International University, agreed to analyze the election on NTN24, a television news network based in Colombia. He found himself on the air debating Omar Bula Escobar, a former United Nations representative notorious for, among other false claims, saying that Mr. Soros controls the Democratic Party, an anti-Semitic trope embraced by QAnon.
 Dr. Gamarra said he rejected Mr. Bula Escobar’s baseless claims of election fraud and then telephoned the network after the interview had concluded.
 “I said: ‘Don’t you ever do that to me again. Do background research on your guests,’” he said. “This is irresponsible.”
 A producer for the show did not immediately respond to a request for comment on Wednesday.
 Evelyn Pérez-Verdía, a Democratic Latino issues strategist in South Florida, said she spent four hours on Tuesday trying to debunk false claims on Spanish-language radio that Mr. Biden, a moderate, was a left-wing radical.
 “The Republicans called him a socialist, in those four hours, 20 times,” she said. “And a radical five times, and a Castro-Chavista” — a reference to Fidel Castro of Cuba and Hugo Chávez of Venezuela — “three times. Repeat a lie and repeat it until it becomes the truth.”
title: Facebook和Twitter似乎忽视了自周二以来针对讲西班牙语的美国人关于选举的大量虚假信息
title_en: Facebook and Twitter appear to have overlooked the deluge of disinformation targeting Spanish-speaking Americans about the election since Tuesday
